Tarea 6 - Correlación y Regresión Lineal (XOM - HAL, COP, VLO, GOOGL) y RLM (agregando dos acciones más), y sus datos comparativos Bloomberg vs getSymbols - yahoo

Ariadna Alonso

Clase 6

#Cargar Librerias

library(quantmod)
library(PerformanceAnalytics)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggcorrplot)
#Descargar datos con getSymbols

# Tickers
tickers <- c("HAL", "XOM", "COP", "VLO", "GOOGL", "TSLA", "AMZN")

# Rango de fechas
from_date <- "2024-02-19"
to_date   <- "2026-02-18"

# Descargar datos
getSymbols(tickers,
           src  = "yahoo",
           from = from_date,
           to   = to_date)
## [1] "HAL"   "XOM"   "COP"   "VLO"   "GOOGL" "TSLA"  "AMZN"
#Construcción de precios ajustados y rendimientos
#Bloomberg usa **returns**, no precios → usamos **log returns**.

# Precios ajustados
prices <- merge(Ad(HAL), Ad(XOM), Ad(COP), Ad(VLO), Ad(GOOGL), Ad(TSLA), Ad(AMZN))
colnames(prices) <- c("HAL", "XOM", "COP", "VLO", "GOOGL", "TSLA", "AMZN" )

# Eliminar NA
prices <- na.omit(prices)

# Rendimientos logarítmicos
returns <- na.omit(Return.calculate(prices, method = "log"))

head(returns)
##                      HAL           XOM          COP         VLO         GOOGL
## 2024-02-21  0.0000000000  0.0202318229  0.029946977  0.01557836  0.0100823658
## 2024-02-22  0.0048272803 -0.0008587966 -0.005775545  0.01899454  0.0107453328
## 2024-02-23 -0.0025527227 -0.0088206849 -0.006346909  0.01279509 -0.0009025367
## 2024-02-26 -0.0085568307  0.0039406077  0.008127628  0.02448912 -0.0454025314
## 2024-02-27  0.0014311181 -0.0021126429 -0.007231167 -0.01464946  0.0094772216
## 2024-02-28 -0.0002858532  0.0027839090  0.003845209 -0.02672099 -0.0181649911
##                    TSLA         AMZN
## 2024-02-21  0.005199146  0.008996960
## 2024-02-22  0.013463406  0.034913390
## 2024-02-23 -0.027943687  0.002345761
## 2024-02-26  0.037973711 -0.001486960
## 2024-02-27  0.001653606 -0.006833819
## 2024-02-28  0.011499231 -0.002192038
#Correlación lineal y Modelo lineal simple

correlation_HAL_XOM <- cor(returns$HAL, returns$XOM)
correlation_HAL_XOM
##           XOM
## HAL 0.7125949
correlation_XOM_COP <- cor(returns$XOM, returns$COP)
correlation_XOM_COP
##           COP
## XOM 0.7949975
correlation_XOM_VLO <- cor(returns$XOM, returns$VLO)
correlation_XOM_VLO
##           VLO
## XOM 0.6122434
correlation_XOM_GOOGL <- cor(returns$XOM, returns$GOOGL)
correlation_XOM_GOOGL
##          GOOGL
## XOM 0.05205445
model_HAL_XOM <- lm(XOM ~ HAL, data = returns)
summary(model_HAL_XOM)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = XOM ~ HAL, data = returns)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.047594 -0.005070  0.000459  0.005885  0.051264 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.0008332  0.0004405   1.892   0.0591 .  
## HAL         0.4274769  0.0188784  22.644   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.009839 on 497 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.5078, Adjusted R-squared:  0.5068 
## F-statistic: 512.7 on 1 and 497 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
model_XOM_COP <- lm(XOM ~ COP, data = returns)
summary(model_XOM_COP)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = XOM ~ COP, data = returns)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.040530 -0.004573 -0.000231  0.005231  0.024963 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.0007842  0.0003808   2.059     0.04 *  
## COP         0.5944834  0.0203473  29.217   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.008507 on 497 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.632,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.6313 
## F-statistic: 853.6 on 1 and 497 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
model_XOM_VLO <- lm(XOM ~ VLO, data = returns)
summary(model_XOM_VLO)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = XOM ~ VLO, data = returns)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.043206 -0.006575  0.000388  0.006843  0.028224 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) 0.0004972  0.0004968   1.001    0.317    
## VLO         0.3918786  0.0227010  17.263   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.01109 on 497 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3748, Adjusted R-squared:  0.3736 
## F-statistic:   298 on 1 and 497 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
model_XOM_GOOGL <- lm(XOM ~ GOOGL, data = returns)
summary(model_XOM_GOOGL)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = XOM ~ GOOGL, data = returns)
## 
## Residuals:
##       Min        1Q    Median        3Q       Max 
## -0.074114 -0.007954  0.000355  0.009337  0.044337 
## 
## Coefficients:
##              Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept) 0.0007810  0.0006291   1.242    0.215
## GOOGL       0.0388710  0.0334503   1.162    0.246
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.01401 on 497 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.00271,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.000703 
## F-statistic:  1.35 on 1 and 497 DF,  p-value: 0.2458
#Gráfica de Correlacion Lineal (tipo Bloomberg)

#HAL/XOM
ggplot(as.data.frame(returns),
       aes(x = XOM, y = HAL)) +
  geom_point(color = "gold", alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = FALSE,
              color = "red",
              linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "HAL vs XOM – Linear Correlation",
    subtitle = "Log returns | 19-Feb-2024 to 18-Feb-2026",
    x = "XOM log returns",
    y = "HAL log returns"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#Insertar imagen Bloomberg
knitr::include_graphics("XOM_HAL.png")

#Gráfica de Correlacion Lineal (tipo Bloomberg)

#XOM/COP
ggplot(as.data.frame(returns),
       aes(x = COP, y = XOM)) +
  geom_point(color = "gold", alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = FALSE,
              color = "red",
              linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "XOM vs COP – Linear Correlation",
    subtitle = "Log returns | 19-Feb-2024 to 18-Feb-2026",
    x = "COP log returns",
    y = "XOM log returns"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#Insertar imagen Bloomberg
knitr::include_graphics("XOM_COP.png")

#Gráfica de Correlacion Lineal (tipo Bloomberg)

#XOM/VLO
ggplot(as.data.frame(returns),
       aes(x = VLO, y = XOM)) +
  geom_point(color = "gold", alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = FALSE,
              color = "red",
              linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "XOM vs VLO – Linear Correlation",
    subtitle = "Log returns | 19-Feb-2024 to 18-Feb-2026",
    x = "VLO log returns",
    y = "XOM log returns"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#Insertar imagen Bloomberg
knitr::include_graphics("XOM_VLO.png")

#Gráfica de Correlacion Lineal (tipo Bloomberg)

#XOM/GOOGL
ggplot(as.data.frame(returns),
       aes(x = GOOGL, y = XOM)) +
  geom_point(color = "gold", alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm",
              se = FALSE,
              color = "red",
              linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "XOM vs GOOGL – Linear Correlation",
    subtitle = "Log returns | 19-Feb-2024 to 18-Feb-2026",
    x = "GOOGL log returns",
    y = "XOM log returns"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

#Insertar imagen Bloomberg
knitr::include_graphics("XOM_GOOGL.png")

#matriz de correlacion
corr_matrix <- cor(returns)
round(corr_matrix, 3)
##         HAL   XOM   COP   VLO GOOGL  TSLA  AMZN
## HAL   1.000 0.713 0.755 0.608 0.220 0.274 0.312
## XOM   0.713 1.000 0.795 0.612 0.052 0.115 0.115
## COP   0.755 0.795 1.000 0.627 0.149 0.221 0.259
## VLO   0.608 0.612 0.627 1.000 0.179 0.208 0.251
## GOOGL 0.220 0.052 0.149 0.179 1.000 0.426 0.534
## TSLA  0.274 0.115 0.221 0.208 0.426 1.000 0.433
## AMZN  0.312 0.115 0.259 0.251 0.534 0.433 1.000

Comparacion con Bloomberg

#Insertar imagen Bloomberg
knitr::include_graphics("Correlacion multiple.png")

ggcorrplot(
  corr_matrix,
  method = "square",
  type   = "lower",
  lab    = TRUE,
  lab_size = 3,
  colors = c("#B2182B", "white", "#2166AC"),
  title  = "Correlation Matrix (Log Returns)",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)
